exact_quote: Located just 60 miles from Spokane International Airport ... receives an average annual snowfall of over 300" ... typically operates from late November through early April
notes: Lodging on-mountain via ski-in/ski-out condos per Chewelah/skimtns sources. XC trails confirmed at Nordic Center. Snowfall numbers vary by source (214 vs 300+); kept the higher figure consistent with snocountry/snow-forecast.

exact_quote: The season at Afton Alps generally begins in late November and runs until early April ... Annual snowfall in the area is about 55 inches, and snowmaking covers 100 percent of the terrain ... The closest major airport to Afton Alps is Minneapolis-St Paul International Airport, about 30 miles away ... Afton opened a snow tubing park ... in December 1997.
notes: Five base chalets but no overnight lodging. Vail-owned.

exact_quote: Alpine Meadows offers the longest season of any Lake Tahoe ski resort with 400 inches of annual snowfall. The season runs from November through mid-May ... Reno-Tahoe International Airport is around 48 miles or a 1-hour drive from Palisades Tahoe ... Alpine Meadows has 13 lifts providing access to 100 designated runs
notes: Part of Palisades Tahoe (formerly Squaw Valley Alpine Meadows). Snowmaking on 9 of 11 lifts per Snow-Forecast. Children's center has tubing.

exact_quote: The nearest airport to Alpine Valley is Detroit Metro, 49 minutes drive away ... Usual opening is late November, and usual closing is Late March, subject to current snow conditions ... All of the trails at Alpine Valley are covered by snowmaking.
notes: Located in White Lake, MI (12 mi west of Pontiac). 25 trails per Snow-Forecast. Drive time given as 49 minutes; specific mileage not stated.

exact_quote: Alpine Valley, the Vail-owned ski resort in Chesterland, will open for the 2025-26 season on Saturday, Dec. 20. Gates open at 9 a.m. ... It is located 40 minutes east of Cleveland by car ... Alpine Valley receives roughly 120 inches of natural snowfall yearly
notes: Cleveland Hopkins (CLE) is nearest airport per news5cleveland. Vail-owned. Drive time 40 min east of Cleveland, exact mileage not given. 60 in average is cited at OnTheSnow; Wikipedia gives 120 natural; chose the higher per Wikipedia.

exact_quote: The nearest airport to Alpine Valley Resort is Milwaukee (MKE) Airport which is 30.5 miles away ... Alpine Valley features over 90 acres of skiable terrain and a vertical drop of 388 feet spread out over 21 runs.
notes: All-season resort with lodging on-property. Located in Elkhorn, WI. Vertical (388 ft) noted instead of summit elevation — used as summit_elevation_ft placeholder is misleading so left null in revision; actually 388 is vertical drop, not summit absolute. Setting summit_elevation_ft to null.

exact_quote: Alta is located 25 miles southeast of Salt Lake City at the top of Little Cottonwood Canyon, approximately 45 minutes from the Salt Lake City International Airport ... Alta has an annual snowfall average of 548 inches ... The search results reference a past opening date of November 22, 2024 ... Alta has a 3-mile groomed track accessible mid-mountain via Sunnyside lift with a Nordic Pass
notes: Skier-only mountain. Alta Lodge etc. provide on-mountain lodging. Backcountry access from Alta is well-documented. Distance is to SLC city center; airport distance ~30 mi (SLC is NW of downtown).

exact_quote: On average, Alyeska Resort receives 650″ at the summit annually ... Alyeska is open from mid-December to mid-April (conditions permitting) ... Located just 40 miles from Anchorage and Ted Stevens Anchorage International Airport ... Alyeska Resort hosts 7 total lifts ... 2 high-speed detachable quads, 2 fixed quads, 2 magic carpets and a 60-passenger Aerial Tram
notes: 304-room Hotel Alyeska provides on-mountain lodging.

exact_quote: Andes Tower Hills has been a local family-owned ski resort since 1980 located in West Central Minnesota, 15 miles west of Alexandria ... has a high-capacity snowmaking system that covers 100% of the ski area ... The resort is open for skiing, snowboarding, tubing and cross-country November-March ... Andes Tower Hills Ski Area in Kensington, Minnesota, opened for the season mid-November.
notes: Closest commercial airport is Alexandria Municipal (AXN); MSP (~150 mi) is the nearest major. Total chairs = 4 (2 triples + 1 quad + 1 surface) excluding tubing lifts and magic carpets.

exact_quote: Angel Fire receives an average of 210 inches of annual snowfall ... The winter season typically spans from mid-December through late March ... The resort is just 24 miles east of Taos and three hours from Albuquerque ... 4 lanes serviced by a conveyor lift offers tubing at the Ski Area ... The Lodge at Angel Fire Resort provides ski-in/ski-out access right at the base
notes: TSM (Taos Regional) is closest commercial; ABQ (Albuquerque Sunport) is ~150 mi for major service. Angel Fire skiing offers around 95 trails across 560 skiable acres per skierdeals.

exact_quote: Antelope Butte is located in the Bighorn National Forest, between Granite Pass and Shell Canyon, sitting just 1:15 from downtown Sheridan. The nearest major airport is Sheridan County Airport, located approximately 40 miles away ... three ski lifts and 250 acres of terrain over 19 trails
notes: Small non-profit ski area. Reliable snowfall Dec-Mar, snow depth ~60 in but annual total not stated. Trail count 19 conflicts with pilot input (40); kept null to avoid mixing.

exact_quote: Located near the cities of La Grande and Baker City in northeastern Oregon, Anthony Lakes has impressive elevation metrics. With a base elevation of 7,100 feet (highest in Oregon) and a summit elevation of 8,000 feet ... The resort receives 300 inches of snowfall annually ... For the 2025-2026 season, the resort was planning to open on December 20 ... one slow speed triple chair, one handle tow and a magic carpet servicing 21 runs
notes: Baker City Muni (BKE) is closest small airport; PDT (Pendleton) ~110 mi for commercial. Wikipedia/snow-forecast give 250 vs 300; chose 300 per Travel Oregon. 21 trails per onelifetoski.

exact_quote: Opening Day is scheduled for November 21 (weather dependent). The average snowfall is 50 inches ... The driving distance between Charlotte Airport (CLT) to Blowing Rock is 88 miles ... there is no tubing at Appalachian ski resort
notes: Appalachian explicitly does not offer tubing. Nearby Hawksnest handles tubing demand.

exact_quote: Apple Mountain is permanently closed. The ski resort is no longer operating for skiing. Following careful consideration, it did not open the ski slopes for the 2017/18 winter season, and it has remained closed since then. However, Apple Mountain offers championship golf, casual dining and a full-service banquet and conference center
notes: DOWNHILL SKI OPERATIONS CLOSED since 2017/18 — flag for dataset cleanup. MBS Intl in Freeland is the local airport for the facility (golf/banquet only now).

exact_quote: Arapahoe Basin announced its opening day for its 79th year would be Sunday, Oct. 26 ... There is no overnight lodging at Arapahoe Basin. The nearest lodging is located at the Keystone ski area, six miles to the west ... There are 3 high-speed lifts ... The resort is served by nine lifts ... summit elevation of 13,050 feet ... a base elevation of 10,520 feet ... gets an average annual snowfall of 265" of snow per year ... Colorado's Arapahoe Basin Extends Ski Season Into Mid-May
notes: Denver (DEN) ~90 mi via I-70; specific mileage not in source. A-Basin known for high-altitude/late-season skiing and backcountry access (Beavers, Steep Gullies).

exact_quote: The area has a base elevation of 2,500 feet (760 m) and rises to almost 4,000 feet (1,200 m) at Rendezvous Peak ... a non-profit ski area just 10 miles from downtown Anchorage ... Arctic Valley has two chair lifts, one platter lift, one rope tow, a tubing lift, and four bowls.
notes: Non-profit weekend-only operation. ANC is ~10 mi from downtown Anchorage; exact airport-to-resort mileage not given. Lift total 4 chairs/surface (2 chair + 1 platter + 1 rope) + tubing — left null since ambiguous categorization.

exact_quote: Arizona Snowbowl is an alpine ski resort located on the San Francisco Peaks of northern Arizona, fifteen miles north of Flagstaff ... The resort receives an average annual snowfall of 260 inches (660 cm) ... For the current 2025-26 season, the Snowbowl opens Friday, Nov. 21 ... The base elevation of Arizona Snowbowl is at 9,200 feet ... Arizona Snowbowl has eight lifts ... Grand Canyon Express: Arizona's first high-speed, six-person lift ... Sleds and tubes are not permitted on Arizona Snowbowl
notes: Basecamp at Snowbowl provides on-mountain cabins. Flagstaff Pulliam (FLG) is closest commercial; PHX (~140 mi) is major hub. Snowbowl owns Agassiz Lodge for summer tubing only.

exact_quote: Ascutney Mountain Resort operated as a downhill ski area from 1946 until 2010 ... Ascutney Mountain now hosts the operations of Ascutney Outdoors, a 501(c)3 organization ... Due to very little snowfall in recent years, the skiing and tubing runs opened for the first time the first weekend in February. Because Ascutney Outdoors relies solely on natural snow
notes: Former Ascutney Mountain Resort closed 2010; reopened as non-profit Ascutney Outdoors with small surface lifts only — no chairlifts, natural snow only. Lebanon Municipal (LEB) is closest local airport in Upper Valley NH/VT.

exact_quote: Aspen Snowmass opened Aspen Highlands and Buttermilk for the 2025-26 winter season on Saturday, Dec. 13, 2025 ... Aspen Highlands receives 300 inches of annual snowfall ... The resort has 5 total lifts consisting of 3 high-speed quads and 2 triple lifts ... 1,040 acres of terrain with 144 trails ... base elevation is 8,040 ft and the summit elevation is 11,675 ft
notes: Highland Bowl hike-to terrain provides legendary inbounds backcountry-style access. ASE (Aspen/Pitkin) is the local commercial airport just minutes from town.

exact_quote: Aspen Mountain and Snowmass are slated to open November 27, 2025 — right on schedule for Thanksgiving week ... Aspen Mountain receives an average of 300 inches of annual snowfall across varied terrain ... the mountains are just 10 minutes from downtown Aspen
notes: ASE (Sardy Field) is the local airport; located in/adjacent to town. Many ski-in/ski-out hotels at base (Little Nell, etc.). Trail map URL confirmed.
